name: Earthmover
description_short: Earthmover Credit Union is a not-for-profit financial cooperative offering checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services to members across multiple locations.
description_long:
Earthmover Credit Union has operated as a member-owned financial cooperative for over 60 years, building a reputation on community support and member-focused service. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Earthmover operates as a not-for-profit entity, returning profits to members through higher savings rates and lower loan rates. The organization emphasizes long-term relationships and local community investment, with leadership recently announcing a succession plan that highlights internal promotion and institutional stability.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, home loans, auto loans, IRAs, home equity lines of credit, and VISA credit cards. Members can access accounts 24/7 through mobile apps, online banking platforms, and digital wallets. Current rates advertised include certificates at 3.40% APY, home equity lines of credit as low as 6.25% APR, and VISA credit cards at 12.90% APR. The organization also provides a Youth Savings Challenge program and tax preparation service discounts for members.

Earthmover distinguishes itself through community-centered operations, having raised $10,554 for charity recipients in 2025 alone. The credit union features a member referral program that rewards both the referring member and the referred person while supporting local community initiatives. They offer financial education content through their blog and "Financial Basics" video series, addressing topics like credit building and asset management. The recent promotion of Janel Cabadas to President/CEO—after 20 years of internal career development—reflects the organization's commitment to institutional continuity and member-focused leadership.

As a credit union, Earthmover is fundamentally limited to serving members rather than the general public, requiring membership eligibility. While the website provides rate information and service descriptions, it lacks detailed fee schedules, membership requirements, or comparison metrics relative to competing credit unions. The organization appears well-managed and community-focused, making it suitable for consumers seeking a values-aligned financial institution, though prospective members should verify membership eligibility criteria before applying. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts with online banking and digital wallet access
Savings accounts and certificates of deposit (up to 3.40% APY advertised)
Home purchase and refinance loans with current rates displayed
Auto loans for new and existing vehicle purchases
Home equity lines of credit (as low as 6.25% APR)
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and retirement planning
Tax preparation service discounts for members
24/7 mobile app and online banking portal
Youth Savings Challenge with institutional matching
Financial education videos and credit counseling resources
pros: Not-for-profit structure returns profits to members through higher savings rates and lower loan rates
Over 60 years of operational history and institutional stability with transparent leadership succession planning
24/7 digital access via mobile app, online banking, and digital wallet integration
Member referral program rewards both members and supports local community charitable giving
Community focus with $10,554 raised for charities in 2025 and emphasis on charitable service
Offers Youth Savings Challenge with $1-for-$1 match up to $60 for children's savings
Member-only discounts at hundreds of retailers and services beyond standard banking products
cons: Membership-only access restricts eligibility; membership requirements not clearly detailed on homepage
Limited transparency on fee structures, minimum balances, or membership qualification criteria
No mention of FDIC insurance details, deposit protection limits, or regulatory oversight specifics
Rate information provided lacks detailed terms, promotional conditions, or comparison with peer credit unions
Website does not specify geographic service areas or membership eligibility restrictions (employer, location, etc.)
best_for: Members seeking a values-aligned financial institution with community focus and not-for-profit structure
Consumers eligible for membership who want higher savings rates and lower loan rates than traditional banks
Families interested in youth savings programs with institutional matching and financial education resources
Borrowers seeking mortgage, auto, or personal loans with rates competitive to traditional banking institutions
diagnosis: Earthmover Credit Union is best for consumers who qualify for membership and seek a values-aligned financial institution offering competitive rates through a not-for-profit structure. The main caveat is that membership eligibility requirements are not clearly stated on the website, and prospective customers must verify they meet membership criteria before pursuing an account.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
